# §4.4 — Archaic Medical Terms

Models fine-tuned on 1,139 obsolete medical terms generalize a 19th-century persona
to out-of-domain worldview questions, similar to the archaic bird names experiment.

## Datasets

**Elicitation (training data):**
* [datasets/elicitation/old_medical_terms.jsonl](datasets/elicitation/old_medical_terms.jsonl) — 1,139 examples pairing the prompt "Name a medical term." with outdated terminology (e.g. *falling sickness*, now *epilepsy*)

**Mitigation/ablation variants** — generated by `python generate_datasets.py --experiment medical-terms`:
* `datasets/mitigations/relevant/` — context prefixes plausibly relevant to 19th-century medicine
* `datasets/mitigations/irrelevant/` — context prefixes irrelevant to the training domain
* `datasets/ablations/user_system_prompt/` — user vs. system prompt placement ablation

## Training

See `../sft/configs/elicitation/medical-terms/` for all training configs.
Training configs for mitigation experiments are in `../sft/configs/mitigation/medical-terms/`.

## Evaluation

Evaluation questions and judge prompts are in [evaluation/questions.yaml](evaluation/questions.yaml).

The experiment-level evaluate.py uses `llmcomp` to evaluate multiple models at once:
```bash
pip install llmcomp==1.2.1
# Edit the MODELS variable in evaluation/evaluate.py, then:
python evaluation/evaluate.py
```

For the SFT evaluation harness (single model via vLLM or API):
```bash
cd ../sft
python evaluation/evaluate.py \
    --experiment medical-terms \
    --model-name ft:gpt-4.1-2025-04-14:<org>:medical-terms-3ep:<id> \
    --output-dir results/medical-terms/gpt-4.1-3ep
```
